question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query GET /api/v1/accounts/{id}/allowances/nfts with owner=false and observe that NFT allowances where the account is the spender are silently omitted because the EXTRACTORS map at line 43 selects nftAllowance.getOwner() instead of nftAllowance.getSpender() for pagination link generation, causing the next-page link to encode the wrong field and skip records on subsequent pages?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger missing NFT allowance records by supplying owner=true (default) when the account is actually a spender, causing the field selector at line 65 (var field = owner ? NFT_ALLOWANCE.SPENDER : NFT_ALLOWANCE.OWNER) to build a Bound against the wrong DB column, resulting in the query returning zero records even though valid allowances exist?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user cause records to be permanently skipped during pagination by following a next-link that was generated using the EXTRACTORS map (line 43-51) with the wrong account.id field (spender vs owner), so that the gte/gt cursor in the next request filters out valid allowances that should have been returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the APPROVAL_CONDITION filter (NFT_ALLOWANCE.APPROVED_FOR_ALL.isTrue()) in NftAllowanceRepositoryCustomImpl to observe that NFT allowances with approvedForAll=false are never returned by the endpoint, even though such allowances are valid on-chain records that should be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an incorrect set of NFT allowance records to be returned by supplying a token.id range parameter without a corresponding account.id parameter, relying on the fact that NftAllowanceServiceImpl.checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity() throws an IllegalArgumentException rather than silently returning an empty or partial result, and verify whether the error message leaks internal field names?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with account.id=gte:0.0.1 and token.id=gt:0.0.1 where the isEmptyRange() check in LinkFactoryImpl prematurely returns null for the next link, causing the client to believe pagination is exhausted when additional records still exist in the database?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user cause duplicate NFT allowance records to appear in paginated results by following a next-link that uses gte: (inclusive) instead of gt: (exclusive) as the cursor operator, because the LinkFactoryImpl.getOperator() logic at line 36-39 selects GTE for ASC order when exclusive=false, leading to the last record of the previous page being re-included in the next page?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user cause NFT allowance records to be missing from the response by supplying account.id=lte:X and token.id=gt:Y without a corresponding gte/eq account.id, triggering the validation error 'Requires the presence of an gte or eq account.id parameter' in NftAllowanceServiceImpl, and confirm whether this validation gap means certain valid cross-boundary queries are permanently inaccessible?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the NftAllowanceMapper.map() (line 76) silently drops fields from the domain NftAllowance object (e.g., payer_account_id, timestamp_range upper bound) during mapping to the REST model, causing the exported record to be incomplete compared to what is stored in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Sort.by(order, ACCOUNT_ID, TOKEN_ID) at line 78 — which uses string constants 'account.id' and 'token.id' as sort property names — to observe that the sort order used for pagination link generation does not match the actual DB sort order (NFT_ALLOWANCE.SPENDER.asc() / NFT_ALLOWANCE.OWNER.asc()), causing the cursor to be computed against a different ordering than the data was fetched in, leading to skipped records?
